backlog.title: No-Shadow Pivot — Explicit agent-browser-pool Invocation
backlog.description: Pivot from the PATH-shadowing wrapper model (Plan 001) to explicit `agent-browser-pool <verb> <args>` invocation (PRD Decision O5). The existing lib/pool.sh core (lease I/O, acquire, release, reap, Chrome launch, exhaustion) is reused unchanged. Changes focus on: (1) making bin/agent-browser-pool the SOLE entry point that handles BOTH pool verbs AND driving commands; (2) removing the POOL_DISABLE safety valve and the no-pi-ancestor passthrough; (3) adding a preflight check for the real agent-browser binary; (4) deleting the old bin/agent-browser shim; (5) rewriting install.sh, SKILL.md, README.md, and tests for the no-shadow model.

milestones.title: Core Library Cleanup (lib/pool.sh)
milestones.description: Remove the POOL_DISABLE mechanism, change the no-pi-ancestor behavior from passthrough to fail-fast, and add a preflight check for the real agent-browser binary. All changes are in the shared library that every downstream component depends on.

tasks.title: Remove POOL_DISABLE mechanism and update no-pi-ancestor behavior
tasks.description: Remove all references to AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE / POOL_DISABLE from pool_config_init and pool_wrapper_main. Change pool_wrapper_main's no-pi-ancestor handling from passthrough (exec real binary) to pool_die fail-fast.

subtasks.title: Remove POOL_DISABLE from pool_config_init and comments
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §1a. POOL_DISABLE is initialized in pool_config_init (lib/pool.sh lines 183-186) and documented in a comment block at line 109. The PRD §2.11 and §2.17 explicitly state: '(removed) AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE and the ~/scripts PATH-shadow are gone — there is no interception to bypass.'
2. INPUT: The existing pool_config_init function (lib/pool.sh:132-210). It reads `AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE` via `_pool_config_bool` and stores it in global `POOL_DISABLE`.
3. LOGIC: In pool_config_init (lib/pool.sh):
   a. Delete the line `disable="$(_pool_config_bool "${AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE:-}")"` (line ~183).
   b. Delete the line `POOL_DISABLE="$disable"; declare -g POOL_DISABLE` (line ~186).
   c. In the comment block at line ~109, remove the row referencing `AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE → POOL_DISABLE`.
   d. Update any other comment in pool_config_init's vicinity that references POOL_DISABLE or the 'safety valve'.
   e. Do NOT remove the `headless` or `allow_slow_copy` boolean lines — those are still needed.
   f. Run `bash -n lib/pool.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash lib/pool.sh` to verify no syntax/lint errors.
4. OUTPUT: pool_config_init no longer sets POOL_DISABLE. The global `POOL_DISABLE` is no longer declared. Any code that reads `$POOL_DISABLE` will see an empty string (unset) — but that code will be removed in P2.M1.T1.S2.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the comment block in pool_config_init that lists env vars to remove the AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE row. No external doc files change here.

subtasks.title: Remove POOL_DISABLE passthrough + change no-pi-ancestor from passthrough to fail-fast in pool_wrapper_main
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §1b. pool_wrapper_main (lib/pool.sh:3601-3740) has two obsolete behaviors: (a) step b checks POOL_DISABLE==1 for passthrough (lines ~3611-3617), and (b) step d: no pi ancestor → passthrough exec (lines ~3631-3635). PRD §2.4 step 1 says: 'No pi ancestor → DRIVING fails fast (requires a pi ancestor; for raw browser use call agent-browser directly)'. PRD §2.11/§2.17: AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE is removed.
2. INPUT: pool_wrapper_main from lib/pool.sh (as modified by P2.M1.T1.S1 which removed POOL_DISABLE from pool_config_init). The function's step b (DISABLE check) and step d (no-pi-ancestor passthrough).
3. LOGIC: In pool_wrapper_main (lib/pool.sh):
   a. DELETE step b entirely (lines ~3611-3617): the `if [[ "${POOL_DISABLE:-0}" == "1" ]]; then ... exec ... fi` block.
   b. CHANGE step d (lines ~3631-3635): Replace the `exec "$POOL_REAL_BIN" "$@"` passthrough with:
      `pool_die "agent-browser-pool: driving commands require a pi ancestor (owning pi process)." "For raw browser use without pooling, call 'agent-browser' directly."`
   c. Update the comment at line ~3631 from 'no pi ancestor → passthrough (human terminal)' to 'no pi ancestor → fail-fast'.
   d. Update all GOTCHA/consumer comments referencing POOL_DISABLE (lines ~3585, ~3598, ~3606): remove 'POOL_DISABLE' from the list of globals consumed by pool_wrapper_main.
   e. Keep pool_dispatch_classify and its meta-passthrough UNCHANGED — meta commands (skills, --version, session list) still pass through to the real binary. This is correct: those commands don't need a lane.
   f. Run `bash -n lib/pool.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash lib/pool.sh`.
4. OUTPUT: pool_wrapper_main no longer checks POOL_DISABLE. A caller without a pi ancestor gets pool_die (exit 1) instead of silent passthrough. The function's consumers (bin/agent-browser-pool driving branch, tests) see the new fail-fast behavior.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update pool_wrapper_main's header comment to remove POOL_DISABLE from the CONSUMES list and reflect the no-pi-ancestor fail-fast. No external doc files change here.

tasks.title: Add preflight check for agent-browser binary on driving calls
tasks.description: PRD §2.16 requires 'a preflight in the pool entry on every driving call that fails fast with an actionable message rather than booting a lane it can't drive.' Add a _pool_preflight_real_bin() function and call it in pool_wrapper_main before lane logic.

subtasks.title: Add _pool_preflight_real_bin() function and call it in pool_wrapper_main
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/system_context.md Gap 5. The pool calls agent-browser by absolute path ($POOL_REAL_BIN, frozen by pool_config_init). Currently there is NO check for its existence before attempting to acquire/boot a lane. PRD §2.16: 'Enforced two ways: (a) doctor's [binary] check; (b) a preflight in the pool entry on every driving call that fails fast.'
2. INPUT: POOL_REAL_BIN global (frozen by pool_config_init, lib/pool.sh:155). The existing pool_wrapper_main function (lib/pool.sh:3601+) as modified by P2.M1.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC:
   a. Add a new function `_pool_preflight_real_bin()` (place it just before pool_wrapper_main, around lib/pool.sh:3590):
      - Check `[[ -f "$POOL_REAL_BIN" && -x "$POOL_REAL_BIN" ]]`.
      - If NOT: `pool_die "agent-browser-pool: the real agent-browser binary is missing or not executable:" "$POOL_REAL_BIN" "Install agent-browser >= 0.28, or set AGENT_BROWSER_REAL to the correct path."`
      - Return 0 on success.
   b. Call `_pool_preflight_real_bin` in pool_wrapper_main AFTER pool_config_init + pool_state_init (step a) but BEFORE the pool_dispatch_classify call (step c). This way it runs on EVERY invocation that reaches pool_wrapper_main (driving commands + meta passthrough). Add it as step 'a.5' or fold it into step a.
   c. The call should be unguarded (pool_die exits on failure; no `if` needed). Pattern: `_pool_preflight_real_bin` on its own line between pool_state_init and pool_dispatch_classify.
   d. Run `bash -n lib/pool.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash lib/pool.sh`.
4. OUTPUT: A _pool_preflight_real_bin() function that validates the binary exists. pool_wrapper_main calls it before any lane logic. A driving call with a missing agent-browser binary fails immediately with an actionable message instead of booting a useless lane.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a docstring comment for _pool_preflight_real_bin documenting the PRD §2.16 enforcement. No external doc files change here.

tasks.title: Update pool_admin_help text for new model
tasks.description: Remove AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE from help output, add driving command documentation, and update the description to reflect that agent-browser-pool handles both pool verbs and driving commands.

subtasks.title: Update pool_admin_help: remove DISABLE, add driving commands section
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §1c. pool_admin_help (lib/pool.sh:4578-4613) currently lists AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE in its config section (line ~4609) and only documents admin verbs (status/reap/release/doctor/help). The description says 'manage the agent-browser ephemeral-profile pool' — it should also mention driving commands.
2. INPUT: The existing pool_admin_help function (lib/pool.sh:4578-4613).
3. LOGIC: In pool_admin_help:
   a. DELETE the line: `printf '  AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE      disable pooling (passthrough) if set (1/true/yes/on)\n'` (line ~4609).
   b. UPDATE the description line (line ~4579) from 'manage the agent-browser ephemeral-profile pool' to something like: 'agent-browser-pool — the sole entry point for browser pool verbs AND driving commands.'
   c. ADD a 'Driving commands' section after the admin Commands section. Document that any token not matching status/reap/release/doctor/help is treated as a driving command (open, click, screenshot, get, etc.) and routes to the caller's locked lane. Give 2-3 examples: `agent-browser-pool open <url>`, `agent-browser-pool screenshot`, `agent-browser-pool close`.
   d. UPDATE the AGENT_CHROME_MASTER description to say 'default: your real Chrome user-data-dir (~/.config/google-chrome)' instead of 'your real Chrome profile'.
   e. Run `bash -n lib/pool.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash lib/pool.sh`.
4. OUTPUT: pool_admin_help output no longer mentions AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE. It documents both admin verbs and driving commands. The help is accurate for the new explicit-invocation model.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The help text IS the documentation change (it's user-facing output). No separate doc files.

milestones.title: Entry Point & Binary Pivot
milestones.description: Make bin/agent-browser-pool the SOLE entry point by adding driving-command dispatch to its case statement. Remove the old bin/agent-browser PATH-shadowing shim entirely.

tasks.title: Update bin/agent-browser-pool dispatch to route driving commands
tasks.description: Change the '*' branch in bin/agent-browser-pool's case statement from erroring ('Unknown command') to calling pool_wrapper_main, making it handle both pool verbs and driving commands as the sole entry point.

subtasks.title: Change '*' branch from error to pool_wrapper_main dispatch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §2. The current bin/agent-browser-pool (25 lines) has a case statement where '*' errors with 'Unknown command: $cmd'. PRD §2.4 step 0: 'anything else → DRIVING command → lane logic below.' PRD §2.12: '<driving verb> [args] # anything else → acquire/reuse MY lane + exec the real agent-browser.'
2. INPUT: bin/agent-browser-pool as it exists now. It sources lib/pool.sh, calls pool_config_init + pool_state_init, then dispatches via case. pool_wrapper_main (from P2.M1.T1.S2 + P2.M1.T2.S1) is ready to handle driving commands.
3. LOGIC: In bin/agent-browser-pool:
   a. Change the '*' case arm from:
      `*) echo "Unknown command: $cmd" >&2; exit 1 ;;`
      to:
      `*) pool_wrapper_main "$@" ;;`
   b. Keep ALL other case arms (status/reap/release/doctor/--help|-h|help) UNCHANGED.
   c. Keep the `cmd="${1:-status}"` default — an bare `agent-browser-pool` with no args still shows status.
   d. The `$@` passed to pool_wrapper_main includes ALL original args (the driving verb + its args). This is critical: pool_wrapper_main needs the full argv to classify and clean.
   e. Update the file header comment from 'admin CLI' to 'SOLE entry point: pool verbs + driving router'.
   f. Run `bash -n bin/agent-browser-pool` and `shellcheck -s bash bin/agent-browser-pool`.
4. OUTPUT: bin/agent-browser-pool dispatches both admin verbs AND driving commands. A call like `agent-browser-pool open https://example.com` reaches pool_wrapper_main which acquires a lane and execs the real agent-browser.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the file header comment in bin/agent-browser-pool to reflect it is now the sole entry point (not just admin CLI). Cross-reference PRD §2.1 and §2.4.

tasks.title: Remove the old PATH-shadowing shim (bin/agent-browser)
tasks.description: Delete bin/agent-browser — the old PATH-shadowing wrapper shim. In the new model, ~/.local/bin/agent-browser is the REAL Vercel CLI (unshadowed), and there is no repo-level wrapper shim.

subtasks.title: Delete bin/agent-browser and update bin/.gitkeep
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §3. bin/agent-browser is a 10-line shim that sources lib/pool.sh and calls pool_wrapper_main "$@". It was the PATH-shadowing entry point for the old model. PRD §2.1 shows ~/.local/bin/agent-browser as 'the REAL Vercel CLI — hard runtime dependency (unchanged, upgradable)'. PRD §3 (Repository layout) does NOT list a bin/agent-browser file.
2. INPUT: The existing bin/agent-browser file. The new bin/agent-browser-pool dispatch from P2.M2.T1.S1 which makes it the sole entry point.
3. LOGIC:
   a. DELETE the file `bin/agent-browser`.
   b. Verify `bin/agent-browser-pool` still exists and is executable (it is the sole entry point).
   c. Check if any code in lib/pool.sh references `bin/agent-browser` by relative path (it doesn't — the shim resolves its own path via readlink -f). No code changes needed in pool.sh.
   d. Check if bin/.gitkeep exists (it does) — keep it so the bin/ directory is tracked by git.
   e. Run `bash -n bin/agent-browser-pool` to verify the remaining binary is still valid.
4. OUTPUT: bin/agent-browser no longer exists. The sole entry point is bin/agent-browser-pool. The real agent-browser at ~/.local/bin/agent-browser is unshadowed.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] No doc file changes needed here — the README/SKILL.md/install.sh updates in later milestones will reflect this deletion. This subtask just removes the file.

milestones.title: Install Script Rewrite
milestones.description: Completely rewrite install.sh for the no-shadow model: three benign things — symlink agent-browser-pool, pre-create state dir, run doctor. No PATH shadowing, no ~/scripts, no cutover warning, no AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE.

tasks.title: Rewrite install.sh for no-shadow model
tasks.description: Replace the 221-line PATH-shadowing cutover installer with a ~60-line benign installer per PRD §2.17.

subtasks.title: Complete rewrite of install.sh — no PATH shadowing, no cutover
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §4 and external_deps.md. The current install.sh (221 lines) does: (a) cutover warning + YES confirmation, (b) symlink bin/agent-browser → ~/scripts/agent-browser (PATH shadow), (c) symlink bin/agent-browser-pool → ~/.local/bin/agent-browser-pool, (d) PATH-ordering verification, (e) pre-create state dir, (f) run doctor. PRD §2.17: 'install.sh does three benign things: 1. symlinks bin/agent-browser-pool → ~/.local/bin/agent-browser-pool (the sole entry point); 2. pre-creates the pool state dir; 3. runs doctor.' Also: 'Removed: the AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE safety valve and the ~/scripts-ahead-of-~/.local/bin PATH requirement.'
2. INPUT: The existing install.sh. The updated bin/agent-browser-pool (sole entry point, from P2.M2). lib/pool.sh (pool_config_init + pool_state_init + pool_admin_doctor).
3. LOGIC: Write a new install.sh that:
   a. Resolves REPO_DIR via readlink -f (same pattern as current).
   b. Validates repo files exist: bin/agent-browser-pool + lib/pool.sh. (Do NOT check for bin/agent-browser — it's deleted.)
   c. Sources lib/pool.sh, calls pool_config_init.
   d. mkdir -p $HOME/.local/bin (idempotent).
   e. ln -sfnv $REPO_DIR/bin/agent-browser-pool $HOME/.local/bin/agent-browser-pool.
   f. pool_state_init (pre-create lanes/ + acquire.lock).
   g. Run doctor: $REPO_DIR/bin/agent-browser-pool doctor.
   h. Print a simple success message (NOT a cutover warning).
   i. Support --force/-f flag (skip nothing — there's no confirmation to skip, but keep it for backward compat / scripted use).
   j. Support --help/-h.
   k. NO ~/scripts symlink. NO PATH-ordering check. NO cutover warning. NO confirmation gate. NO AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE references.
   l. Run `bash -n install.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash install.sh`.
4. OUTPUT: A clean ~50-70 line install.sh that does exactly three things. Installing cannot disrupt running agents (no PATH interception).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] install.sh's own output IS the install documentation (Mode A per §5). The success message should tell the user: (1) the symlink was created, (2) doctor status, (3) how to use `agent-browser-pool`. Also mention uninstall: `rm -f ~/.local/bin/agent-browser-pool`.

milestones.title: Skill & Reference Documentation
milestones.description: Rewrite the agent-facing skill docs (SKILL.md, configuration.md, README.md) to teach `agent-browser-pool <verb>` as the sole command. Remove all PATH-shadowing and AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE references.

tasks.title: Rewrite SKILL.md for explicit invocation model
tasks.description: Complete rewrite of .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/SKILL.md. All examples change from `agent-browser <verb>` to `agent-browser-pool <verb>`. Remove transparent-wrapper language. Teach the invariant command model.

subtasks.title: Complete rewrite of SKILL.md — agent-browser-pool as sole command
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §5. The current SKILL.md (~125 lines) teaches `agent-browser` (PATH-shadowing). Its frontmatter description says 'Drive a browser through the agent-browser transparent pool wrapper'. All examples use `agent-browser open <url>` etc. It discusses passthrough (no pi ancestor, AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE).
2. INPUT: The finalized entry point (bin/agent-browser-pool from P2.M2.T1.S1) and pool_wrapper_main behavior (fail-fast no-pi-ancestor from P2.M1.T1.S2). PRD §2.15 (Invocation checklist — the contract the skill teaches).
3. LOGIC: Rewrite SKILL.md:
   a. Frontmatter: description → 'Drive a browser through agent-browser-pool — get a dedicated, isolated Chrome profile lane, reuse it across calls, and tear it down correctly. Use whenever you run agent-browser-pool to open pages, connect, close, scrape, or automate.'
   b. Title: 'Agent Browser Pool — how to use your Chrome lane'
   c. Opening paragraph: Explain that `agent-browser-pool <verb> <args>` is the INVARIANT command — the same every time, lane selected by process identity, never an argument. NOT a transparent wrapper — an explicit tool.
   d. Section 1 'Get + connect': `agent-browser-pool open https://example.com` (not `agent-browser open ...`). Emphasize: 'The command never names a lane.'
   e. Connection rules: Same as before but with agent-browser-pool examples. `agent-browser-pool connect <port>` → arg stripped. `agent-browser-pool --session <X> ...` → overridden.
   f. Section 'Which commands trigger a lane': Driving commands (open, connect, close, get, screenshot, ...) acquire/use your lane. Meta commands (skills, --version, session list, dashboard, plugin, mcp) pass through. BUT: no mention of AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE (removed) and no mention of 'no pi ancestor → passthrough' (it now fails fast).
   g. Section 2 'Tear down': `agent-browser-pool close` → disconnect-only. Release happens automatically when pi exits.
   h. Section 3 'Safety': Never enter credentials, verify URLs, never drive the source profile directly.
   i. Remove ALL references to: 'transparent PATH-shadowing wrapper', AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE, 'human terminal passthrough'.
   j. Use PRD §2.15 invocation checklist as the structural guide.
4. OUTPUT: A SKILL.md that correctly teaches `agent-browser-pool <verb> <args>` as the sole browser command. An agent reading it would type `agent-browser-pool open <url>`, not `agent-browser open <url>`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] SKILL.md IS the documentation artifact. This is a Mode A change (user-facing contract doc). No separate docs subtask needed for this file.

tasks.title: Update configuration.md reference
tasks.description: Remove AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE, update AGENT_CHROME_MASTER default, update dispatch table and troubleshooting matrix for the no-shadow model.

subtasks.title: Update configuration.md — remove DISABLE, fix master default, update dispatch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §6. The current configuration.md (~170 lines) has: (a) `AGENT_CHROME_MASTER` default listed as `~/.agent-chrome-profiles/master-profile` (WRONG — should be real Chrome dir), (b) `AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE` row in env table + a callout section, (c) dispatch table item 1 = 'AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE truthy → passthrough', (d) troubleshooting row referencing DISABLE.
2. INPUT: The finalized pool_config_init defaults (POOL_MASTER_DIR = real Chrome dir), the new dispatch model (pool verbs vs driving, no DISABLE).
3. LOGIC: In references/configuration.md:
   a. UPDATE the `AGENT_CHROME_MASTER` row: default → `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-~/.config}/google-chrome` (your real Chrome user-data-dir). Update the meaning column to reflect 'may be live/in-use (PRD §2.7)'.
   b. DELETE the `AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE` row from the env table.
   c. DELETE the callout about AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE being 'the safety valve'.
   d. UPDATE 'The three that most affect behavior' section: remove DISABLE, replace with AGENT_CHROME_MASTER (now defaults to live Chrome, so agents pick up new auth).
   e. UPDATE the 'Command dispatch' section: remove item 1 (DISABLE → passthrough). Change item 3 from 'No pi ancestor → passthrough' to 'No pi ancestor → fail-fast (pool_die with guidance)'. The order becomes: (1) meta command → passthrough, (2) no pi ancestor → fail-fast, (3) otherwise → acquire/find lane.
   f. UPDATE troubleshooting matrix: remove the 'Wrong browser / no lane acquired' row's DISABLE reference. Change fix to: 'Run under pi; for raw browser use call agent-browser directly.'
   g. UPDATE 'How acquire works' section: change `agent-browser open <url>` to `agent-browser-pool open <url>`.
   h. Run shellcheck on any embedded bash snippets.
4. OUTPUT: configuration.md is accurate for the new model. No DISABLE references. Master default is correct. Dispatch table reflects pool-verb-vs-driving model.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] configuration.md IS the documentation artifact.

tasks.title: Update skill README.md
tasks.description: Update .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/README.md to remove transparent-wrapper language and DISABLE references.

subtasks.title: Update skill README.md for new model
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §7. The skill README.md (~50 lines) describes the skill as teaching 'transparent Chrome-profile wrapper'. It mentions 'passthrough (no pi ancestor / AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE)' as a pitfall. It uses `agent-browser` in examples.
2. INPUT: The updated SKILL.md from P2.M4.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: In .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/README.md:
   a. Update the description: remove 'transparent Chrome-profile wrapper', use 'agent-browser-pool Chrome-profile pool' or similar.
   b. Update 'What it covers': change 'first driving agent-browser command' to 'first driving agent-browser-pool command'.
   c. Update pitfalls: remove AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE reference. Change 'no pi ancestor → passthrough' to 'no pi ancestor → fails fast (use agent-browser directly for raw access)'.
   d. Keep the Files section and Installation section largely unchanged (they reference the skill directory structure, not the command).
4. OUTPUT: skill README.md is consistent with the new model.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The README.md IS the documentation artifact.

milestones.title: Test Framework Updates
milestones.description: Update all test files to use bin/agent-browser-pool as the sole entry point for both admin verbs and driving commands. Remove POOL_DISABLE test coverage. Update transparency tests for the fail-fast no-pi-ancestor behavior.

tasks.title: Update test/validate.sh framework
tasks.description: Remove the ABPOOL_WRAPPER variable (points to deleted bin/agent-browser), remove the selftest_config_disable test (tests POOL_DISABLE), and update any references.

subtasks.title: Remove ABPOOL_WRAPPER and DISABLE selftest from validate.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §8. validate.sh defines `ABPOOL_WRAPPER="$ABPOOL_REPO/bin/agent-browser"` (line 26) and `ABPOOL_ADMIN="$ABPOOL_REPO/bin/agent-browser-pool"` (line 27). The selftest function `selftest_config_disable` (lines ~346-357) tests AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE → POOL_DISABLE. Line 314 checks `[[ -x "$ABPOOL_WRAPPER" ]]`.
2. INPUT: The finalized lib/pool.sh (POOL_DISABLE removed, P2.M1.T1.S1). bin/agent-browser is deleted (P2.M2.T2.S1).
3. LOGIC: In test/validate.sh:
   a. DELETE line 26: `ABPOOL_WRAPPER="$ABPOOL_REPO/bin/agent-browser"`. bin/agent-browser no longer exists.
   b. UPDATE line 314 (`[[ -x "$ABPOOL_WRAPPER" ]]` check): Either remove it entirely or change it to check `ABPOOL_ADMIN` instead. Prefer: remove the wrapper-existence check (ABPOOL_ADMIN is already checked elsewhere or should be).
   c. DELETE the `selftest_config_disable` function entirely (lines ~346-357). POOL_DISABLE no longer exists.
   d. UPDATE the selftest suite runner: remove `selftest_config_disable` from the list of selftests that are run.
   e. UPDATE any comment referencing the 'wrapper' or 'cutover' to reference 'sole entry point' or 'explicit invocation' instead.
   f. Run `bash -n test/validate.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash test/validate.sh`.
4. OUTPUT: validate.sh no longer references ABPOOL_WRAPPER or POOL_DISABLE. The selftest suite runs without the DISABLE test.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] No external doc files change. Internal test comments are updated inline.

tasks.title: Update test/transparency.sh for new invocation and fail-fast model
tasks.description: Major update: change all $ABPOOL_WRAPPER invocations to $ABPOOL_ADMIN, update meta-passthrough tests (--help now shows pool help, not real binary help), and change no-pi-ancestor test from passthrough to fail-fast assertion.

subtasks.title: Rewrite transparency.sh invocations + update passthrough/fail-fast tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §9. transparency.sh (502 lines) tests the PRD §2.15 'no-idea contract' with 8 test bodies. Key changes needed: (a) All $ABPOOL_WRAPPER → $ABPOOL_ADMIN (bin/agent-browser-pool); (b) Test (a) `skills get core` → still meta passthrough (via pool_dispatch_classify inside pool_wrapper_main); (c) Test (b) `--help` → NOW shows pool help (caught by dispatch case), NOT real binary help — byte-equal assertion is WRONG. `--version` still passes through. (d) No-pi-ancestor was passthrough, now fail-fast — any test asserting passthrough must assert pool_die instead. (e) Tests (c)-(h) driving commands change $ABPOOL_WRAPPER → $ABPOOL_ADMIN.
2. INPUT: The updated validate.sh framework (P2.M5.T1.S1 — ABPOOL_WRAPPER removed, ABPOOL_ADMIN available). The new pool_wrapper_main (P2.M1.T1.S2 — no-pi-ancestor → pool_die). The new bin/agent-browser-pool dispatch (P2.M2.T1.S1 — driving commands reach pool_wrapper_main).
3. LOGIC: In test/transparency.sh:
   a. Replace ALL `$ABPOOL_WRAPPER` with `$ABPOOL_ADMIN` throughout the file. The _transparency_drive helper (line ~179) should use $ABPOOL_ADMIN.
   b. Test (a) `skills get core`: invocation changes to `$ABPOOL_ADMIN skills get core`. The passthrough still works (pool_dispatch_classify → meta → exec real binary). The byte-equal assertion (`wrapper output == real binary output`) STILL HOLDS — the real binary is still invoked for meta commands. Just rename the variable.
   c. Test (b) `--help`/`--version`: SPLIT this test. `--help` is now a pool verb → pool_admin_help → pool help text (NOT real binary help). The byte-equal assertion MUST be removed for --help. Instead, assert that `--help` output CONTAINS 'agent-browser-pool' (the pool's help). For `--version`: it goes to pool_wrapper_main → pool_dispatch_classify → meta → passthrough. The byte-equal assertion STILL HOLDS for --version.
   d. No-pi-ancestor test: If there's a test asserting passthrough for no-pi-ancestor driving commands (there may not be an explicit one — the comments at line ~108 reference passthrough), update it to assert pool_die (exit 1 + error message containing 'pi ancestor').
   e. Tests (c)-(h): Change invocations from $ABPOOL_WRAPPER to $ABPOOL_ADMIN. Behavior is the same (lane acquire/boot/connected), just different binary path.
   f. Update ALL comments referencing 'wrapper', 'PATH-shadowing', 'passthrough' to reflect the explicit-invocation model.
   g. Run `bash -n test/transparency.sh` and `shellcheck -s bash test/transparency.sh`.
4. OUTPUT: transparency.sh tests pass with the new bin/agent-browser-pool sole entry point. Meta passthrough works for skills/--version. --help shows pool help. No-pi-ancestor fails fast. Driving commands route through agent-browser-pool.
5. MOCKING: Tests use AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_OWNER_PID/_STARTTIME env overrides to simulate pi ancestors (no real pi process needed). AGENT_CHROME_HEADLESS=1 for unattended runs. Tests call lib functions directly (pool_acquire_locked, pool_boot_lane) to avoid terminal exec hangs.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] No external doc files. Internal test comments are updated inline.

RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §10-11. concurrency.sh (440 lines) calls lib functions directly (pool_acquire_locked, pool_boot_lane) — it does NOT invoke the wrapper binary. Its comment at line 12 says 'the wrapper (bin/agent-browser → pool_wrapper_main)'. release_reaper.sh (474 lines) already uses `agent-browser-pool release/reap` for admin verbs. Both reference the 'real agent-browser' binary at ~/.local/bin/agent-browser (which is correct — that's the REAL Vercel CLI).
2. INPUT: No functional changes needed. The test logic is correct. Only language/comments are stale.
3. LOGIC:
   a. In test/concurrency.sh:
      - Line 12: Change 'the wrapper (bin/agent-browser → pool_wrapper_main)' to 'the pool entry point (bin/agent-browser-pool → pool_wrapper_main)'.
      - Any other comment referencing 'wrapper' or 'PATH-shadowing' → update to 'sole entry point' or 'explicit invocation'.
   b. In test/release_reaper.sh:

      - Line 20: 'agent-browser-pool release N' is already correct.

   c. Verify both files still pass `bash -n` and `shellcheck -s bash`.

tasks.title: Rewrite README.md for the no-shadow explicit-invocation model
tasks.description: Complete rewrite of README.md: remove all PATH-shadowing language, change command examples to agent-browser-pool, update installation instructions, remove AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE, update source profile default to real Chrome dir.

subtasks.title: Complete rewrite of README.md for new model
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_97982899bef6/architecture/gap_analysis.md §12. The current README.md (~280 lines) describes the PATH-shadowing model: 'transparent PATH-shadowing wrapper', cutover warning, ~/scripts symlink, AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE safety valve, master-profile source. ALL of this is obsolete.
2. INPUT: The complete pivoted codebase — all P2.M1-M5 subtasks are done. The new install.sh, SKILL.md, configuration.md, and lib/pool.sh are finalized.
3. LOGIC: Rewrite README.md:
   a. Title + tagline: 'agent-browser-pool — dedicated Chrome profile lanes for AI agents, via a single invariant command.' (NOT 'transparent PATH-shadowing wrapper').
   b. Bullet points: (1) 'Not a fork' — thin bash wrapper + agent-browser-pool CLI. (2) 'Ephemeral profiles' — CoW copy from your REAL Chrome profile (default: ~/.config/google-chrome), deleted on release. (3) '1 agent = 1 browser' — mutual exclusion via leases keyed on owning pi process. (4) 'Explicit invariant command' — agents run `agent-browser-pool <verb> <args>`; the lane is selected by process identity, never an argument.
   c. Status: 'MVP V2 — explicit invocation model (no PATH shadowing).'
   d. Prerequisites: (1) btrfs at pool root. (2) Real Chrome profile at ~/.config/google-chrome (or set AGENT_CHROME_MASTER). (3) agent-browser >= 0.28 at ~/.local/bin/agent-browser. (4) google-chrome-stable.
   e. Installation: 'install.sh does three benign things: symlinks bin/agent-browser-pool → ~/.local/bin/agent-browser-pool, pre-creates the state dir, runs doctor.' NO cutover warning. 'Because there is no PATH shadowing, installing cannot disrupt running agents.' Uninstall: `rm -f ~/.local/bin/agent-browser-pool`.
   f. Quick start: `agent-browser-pool open https://example.com` — your lane, same browser for the session.
   g. Commands section: Show agent-browser-pool for BOTH driving commands AND admin verbs. Example table per PRD §2.12.
   h. Configuration: Full env-var table. AGENT_CHROME_MASTER default = real Chrome dir. NO AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE row.
   i. Admin commands: status/reap/release/doctor — same as before but through agent-browser-pool.
   j. Architecture: Describe lib/pool.sh + bin/agent-browser-pool (sole entry point). The real agent-browser is called by absolute path.
   k. Remove ALL references to: 'PATH-shadowing', 'transparent wrapper', 'cutover', '~/scripts', 'AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE', 'safety valve', 'master-profile'.
4. OUTPUT: A README.md that accurately describes the explicit-invocation model. A new user reading it would install via install.sh and use `agent-browser-pool open <url>`.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task (per §5 Mode B). It summarizes the entire pivot delta in the top-level README.
